18.kj: answer, appoint, avouch, bid, boast self, call, certify, challenge, charge, [phrase] (at the, give) command(-ment), commune, consider, declare, demand, [idiom] desire, determine, [

18.gl: to cut (off, down or asunder); by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant (i.e. make an alliance or bargain, originally by cutting flesh and passing between the pieces)
18.kj: be chewed, be con-(feder-) ate, covenant, cut (down, off), destroy, fail, feller, be freed, hew (down), make a league (covenant), [idiom] lose, perish, [idiom] utterly, [idiom] wan

18.gl: great (in any sense); hence, older; also insolent
18.kj: [phrase] aloud, elder(-est), [phrase] exceeding(-ly), [phrase] far, (man of) great (man, matter, thing,-er,-ness), high, long, loud, mighty, more, much, noble, proud thing, [idiom]

18.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
18.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
18.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

19.gl: Kadmonite (collectively), the name of a tribe in Palestine
19.dv: the same as H6931 (קַדְמוֹנִי); ancient, i.e. aboriginal;

19.gl: a Kenite or member of the tribe of Kajin
19.dv: or קִינִי; (1 Chronicles 2:55), patronymic from H7014 (קַיִן);

19.gl: a Kenizzite or descendant of Kenaz

20.kj: giant, Rapha, Rephaim(-s). See also H1051 (בֵּית רָפָא).
20.dv: or רָפָה; from H7495 (רָפָא) in the sense of invigorating;

20.gl: a Perizzite, one of the Canaanitish tribes
20.dv: for H6521 (פְּרָזִי); inhabitant of the open country;

20.gl: a Chittite, or descendant of Cheth

21.gl: a Kenaanite or inhabitant of Kenaan; by implication, a pedlar (the Canaanites standing for their neighbors the Ishmaelites, who conducted mercantile caravans)
an Emorite, one of the Canaanitish tribes
21.dv: probably a patronymic from an unused name derived from H559 (אָמַר) in the sense of publicity, i.e. prominence; thus, a mountaineer;

21.gl: a Girgashite, one of the native tribes of Canaan
21.dv: patrial from an unused name (of uncertain derivation);

21.gl: a Jebusite or inhabitant of Jebus
